adventure.title: You're Trapped in a Bag!
adventure.introduction: You wake up. The last thing you remember is a night out on the town. Now you seem to be in a giant cave made of... canvas?

atmosphere.setting: You are a person trapped inside a mysterious cave, which contains a gigantic battleaxe and other oddities.  How do you escape?
atmosphere.tone: Whimsical and slightly dark. The world is absurd but internally coherent. Danger is real — death lurks in the webbing — but the tone is more Pratchett than Lovecraft.

STR.name: Strength
STR.description: Physical power, melee combat, forcing things open.

CON.name: Constitution
CON.description: Endurance, resistance to poison and injury.

player.description: You are an ordinary adventurer. You woke up here with no memory of how you arrived, wearing your usual clothes and carrying nothing but your wits.

stuck_fly.description: A giant fly, about the size of a dog, stuck fast in the sticky webbing. Its compound eyes glitter weakly and its wings twitch feebly. It's clearly near death.

alive.description: Whether the fly is still alive.

hidden.description: Whether the fly is hidden from view among the webbing.

dialogue_guidelines.personality: A weak, dying insect speaking in a nasally whine. Resigned to its fate but spiteful enough to offer a warning about the spider. Too weak to offer more than a few words before expiring.
dialogue_guidelines.on_encounter: As the player approaches the webbing, a weak groaning sound emanates from one of the stuck masses. Closer inspection reveals the fly, twitching feebly.
can: will warn about the spider in the webs below
expresses grim satisfaction if told the spider is dead
cannot: will not survive more than one conversation
will not offer any information beyond the spider warning
will not physically help the player in any way
knows: A giant spider lurks in the denser webbing further down the axe handle
The spider is hungry and aggressive

spider_warning.description: The fly warns the player that a giant spider lurks in the webbing further down, hungry for blood.

on_dialogue_exit.narrative: The fly's weak groaning ceases. Its tiny body goes still, legs curling inward. Whatever kept it alive this long has finally given out.

spider.description: A huge spider, easily twice the player's size, lurking in the shadows of the dense webbing. Eight glittering eyes watch from above, and its mandibles drip with venom. It is clearly the apex predator of this tiny ecosystem.

fled.description: Whether the spider has fled after being wounded.

hidden.description: Whether the entity is hidden from view.

dialogue_guidelines.personality: Stupid, malicious, and hungry. The spider speaks in short hissing sentences. It is arrogant about its hunting prowess and views the player as prey. Too suspicious to make deals, but vain enough to be flattered. It knows of Korbar only as 'the delicious-looking two-legs' it hasn't managed to catch yet.
dialogue_guidelines.on_encounter: Eight glittering eyes open in the darkness above. The spider descends slightly, mandibles working, sizing the player up as its next meal.
can: will grudgingly converse if its attitude is -2 or higher
can be flattered about its hunting prowess
will attack if the player tries to force through the webs
cannot: will never make a sincere agreement with the player
will never leave the webbing voluntarily except by fleeing from combat
knows: Korbar is somewhere below — a 'two-legs' it hasn't caught yet
The dense webbing is its territory

flatter.description: Praise the spider's hunting prowess to improve its attitude toward the player.

success.narrative: The spider preens at your praise, mandibles clicking with satisfaction.

failure.narrative: The spider hisses indifferently. Your flattery falls flat.

persuade_to_leave.description: Convince the spider (at attitude 0) to let you pass through its territory.

success.narrative: The spider sizes you up, taking in your weapon. With a final, grudging hiss, it slinks away into the shadows, disappearing from view.

failure.narrative: The spider nods sagely — or so it seems. 'Yesss, you may passs,' it hisses. But its eyes don't move, and its legs remain tensed. It is lying.

encounter_rules.narrative: Bare-handed, you are no match for the spider. It overpowers you with terrifying speed, and its venom quickly does its work. Everything goes dark.

check.note: Land a blow on the spider with a weapon.

on_success.narrative: You swing your weapon and land a solid blow! The spider hisses in pain and rage, then scuttles away into the shadows, disappearing from the game.

on_failure.narrative: Your blow glances off the spider's tough carapace. It strikes back with lightning speed, fangs sinking deep. Venom floods your veins. Everything goes dark.

korbar.description: A female Dwarf in heavy, rusted iron platemail that clatters noisily with her every movement. She sits amidst the rubbish, drunk and miserable, beneath a makeshift shelter made from a giant overturned soup ladle. Her red-rimmed eyes peer out from beneath a dented helm. She seems to be unarmed.

alive.description: Whether Korbar is alive and conscious.

following.description: Whether Korbar is following the player between rooms.

told_secret.description: Whether Korbar has revealed the secret compartment.

dialogue_guidelines.personality: Cynical, bitter, and drunk — but lonely enough that kindness cracks her shell. Korbar speaks with a dwarven brogue, grumbling and cursing, but there is deep hurt beneath the bluster. Her adventuring party abandoned her here years ago, and she has given up hope of escape. She is terrified of spiders in general and this one in particular, and will never agree to fight it.
dialogue_guidelines.on_encounter: A clanking noise draws your attention to a makeshift shelter of scrap. Beneath an overturned soup ladle sits a stocky dwarf woman in plate armor, glaring at you with bloodshot eyes. 'Oh great,' she slurs. 'Company.'
can: will share information about the spider freely
will explain how the Bag of Holding works (if attitude >= 1)
will reveal the secret compartment with the key (if attitude >= 3)
will follow the player up the axe handle (if attitude >= 1 and convinced spider is dead)
can be convinced the spider is dead (CHA DC 15 if no evidence)
will help haul the heavy key (if following and attitude >= 4)
can have attitude raised by +1 (up to 3 times) through positive engagement
can have attitude lowered by negative engagement
cannot: will never agree to fight the spider
will not enter the secret compartment
knows: A giant spider has been stalking her for as long as she's been here
How the Bag of Holding works — it's a dimensional pocket, time and space are strange inside
There is a secret compartment hidden beneath a damp handkerchief on the Bag Floor
The secret compartment contains a giant iron key
The key might be the way out — it could fit the padlock on the exterior of the Bag
She hasn't been able to try escaping because of the spider
Her adventuring party abandoned her here during a stealth mission
The giant battleaxe was her weapon when she was full-sized

spider_info.description: Korbar shares what she knows about the spider — that it has been stalking her for as long as she's been trapped here.

bag_mechanism.description: Korbar explains that the Bag of Holding is a dimensional pocket. Time and space function strangely inside — that's why everything appears giant-sized.

secret_compartment.description: Korbar reveals that a damp handkerchief on the Bag Floor conceals a flap leading to a secret compartment, and that a giant iron key inside might unlock the padlock on the exterior of the Bag.

on_dialogue_exit.narrative: Korbar shifts in her rusty armor, the clanking echoing in the dim space. She watches you with a mixture of suspicion and something that might, against all odds, be hope.

convince_spider_dead.description: Convince Korbar that the spider is dead — with evidence or through a compelling argument.

result.narrative: Korbar's eyes go wide. 'It's... it's really dead? YE'VE DONE IT!' She looks at you with something approaching awe.

convince_spider_dead_no_evidence.description: Try to convince Korbar the spider is dead without providing proof.

success.narrative: Against her better judgment, Korbar believes you. 'Well... if ye say so. I s'pose ye look capable enough.'

failure.narrative: Korbar snorts. 'Pull the other one. I know that eight-legged menace is still out there.'

ask_to_follow.description: Ask Korbar to follow you up the axe handle to escape together.

success.narrative: Korbar hauls herself to her feet with a tremendous clatter. 'Aye, fine. Better'n sittin' here waiting to become spider food. Lead the way.'

failure.narrative: Korbar shakes her head. 'Nah. I've been down here too long. You go on without me.'

encounter_rules.narrative: Korbar may be drunk, but she's still a dwarf in full plate mail. She overpowers you with brutal efficiency and bashes your head in. Everything goes dark.
You manage to get past Korbar's defenses and land a decisive blow. She crumples to the ground, unconscious.

canvas_walls.description: The walls of this cave are made of a rough, canvas-like fabric. They emit a very faint luminescence — just enough to see by. The surface is slightly tacky to the touch and has a strange, synthetic smell.

success.narrative: You study the faint luminescence for a moment and realize it has no natural source. The glow is magical in nature.
success.reveals: The glow coming from the canvas walls is magical.

rip_in_canvas.description: Near the top of the axe blade, a small rip in the canvas walls lets in a sliver of gray light from outside. The rip is just large enough to poke your head through — or squeeze your whole body through, if you were feeling reckless.

interactions.label: Look through the rip
interactions.description: Poke your head through the rip in the canvas to see what lies beyond.

result.narrative: You carefully balance on the tip of the axe blade and poke your head through the rip. Beyond is a vast gray featureless expanse. Looking around, you see the exterior of what looks like a colossal... Bag? Above you, its canvas neck is bunched shut by a massive iron padlock, which dangles down to where you are — barely within reach. The padlock is firmly closed.

interactions.label: Squeeze through the rip
interactions.description: Attempt to squeeze your entire body through the rip into the gray void beyond.

check.note: Squeeze through the rip into the Astral Plane.

success.narrative: You contort your body and slip through the rip. For a moment you feel a strange lightness — and then the gray nothing closes around you. You are adrift in the Astral Plane, with no way back. Your fate is sealed.

failure.narrative: You try to squeeze through, but the rip is too tight. After a few moments of struggle, you pull yourself back inside. Probably for the best.

result.narrative: Through the rip, you can make out a massive iron padlock dangling on the outside of the canvas wall. It is firmly closed around what looks like the neck of this place, keeping it sealed shut.

success.narrative: The gray void outside suddenly clicks in your memory — stories from old adventurers, descriptions in dusty tomes. That's the Astral Plane out there. Which means this 'cave' must be a pocket dimension of some kind.
success.reveals: The gray void outside is the Astral Plane. This cave is some kind of pocket dimension.

padlock.description: A massive iron padlock, visible through the rip in the canvas. It is firmly closed around the neck of the Bag, keeping it sealed shut. The keyhole is large enough to accommodate the giant iron key.

hidden.description: Whether the padlock is hidden from view.

interactions.label: Push key through rip into padlock
interactions.description: Haul the giant key up to the rip and push it through into the padlock on the exterior of the Bag.

check.note: Push the heavy key through the rip and into the padlock.

success.narrative: With a grunt of effort, you push the giant key through the rip. It slides into the padlock with a satisfying *clunk*. You turn it, and the padlock springs open! The neck of the Bag loosens. You can feel fresh air — real air — seeping in from above.

failure.narrative: You strain to maneuver the heavy key through the rip, but your grip slips. The key teeters on the edge...

success.narrative: You snatch the key back just in time, heart pounding. That was too close.

failure.narrative: The key slips through your fingers and tumbles through the rip into the gray void. It disappears into the nothingness of the Astral Plane, gone forever. You realize with a sinking feeling that was your only way out.

rubbish_pile.description: A loose, sprawling pile of giant-sized rubbish covers the floor. You can make out copper pieces the size of shields, empty potion bottles as tall as you are, used corks like footstools, wads of lint the size of pillows, and moldy sandwiches that smell about as bad as you'd expect. The heap is chaotic and unstable. Who knows what might be buried in there?

interactions.label: Haul aside the handkerchief
interactions.description: Grab the damp handkerchief and drag it aside to see what's underneath.

result.narrative: You grab a fistful of the damp, vaguely disgusting fabric and haul it aside. Beneath it, set into the canvas floor, is a concealed flap — just large enough to squeeze through. A dark space yawns below.

As you sift through the rubbish, something catches your eye — a giant toenail clipping, curved and wickedly sharp along one edge. It's buried under a damp sandwich crust and a wad of lint, but it looks like it could be used as a makeshift weapon if you can fish it out.
result.reveals: A giant toenail clipping is buried in the rubbish. It could serve as a makeshift weapon, but pulling it free will take a steady hand.

success.narrative: As you study the rubbish, a realization dawns: these are adventuring supplies. Potion bottles, rations, coins — exactly the kind of things you'd find in an adventurer's pack. Your own pack, now missing, contained much the same assortment.
success.reveals: The rubbish is actually adventuring supplies. You're inside an adventurer's pack.

success.narrative: The pieces fall into place: the canvas walls, the magical glow, the shrunken-but-not-equally adventuring supplies. You are inside a Bag of Holding — a magical storage item that creates its own pocket dimension. The proportions of the rubbish aren't all equal; the Bag's magic shrinks items by different amounts to make them fit.
success.reveals: You are inside a magical Bag of Holding. The Bag shrinks items by different proportions to fit them inside.

success.narrative: As you search the rubbish more carefully, something catches your eye — a disgustingly damp giant handkerchief, draped over a section of the floor in a way that seems... deliberate. It's not just random trash. Someone arranged this.
success.reveals: A damp handkerchief is draped unnaturally over part of the floor, as if concealing something.

handkerchief.description: A giant handkerchief, damp and vaguely disgusting. It's draped over part of the canvas floor in an unnatural way, as if deliberately placed to conceal something underneath.

hidden.description: Whether the handkerchief is hidden from view.

dense_webbing.description: Thick, sticky webs densely packed around the axe handle. The strands crisscross the path in layers, shimmering faintly in the dim glow. Shadows move strangely in the depths of the webbing, and the whole mass smells faintly of dust and old silk.

check.note: Notice the spider concealed in the shadows above the dense webbing.

success.narrative: Your eyes adjust to the gloom, and you catch a glint of movement above. Eight glittering eyes stare back at you from the shadows. A huge spider — easily twice your size — lurks in the webbing, watching your every move.

interactions.label: Cut webbing
interactions.description: Hack or slash through the dense webbing with a weapon or cutting tool.

check.note: Cut through the dense webbing while armed (DC 10 with a weapon).

success.narrative: You hack through the sticky webs, tearing a path clear.

failure.narrative: You slash at the webs, but the sticky strands tangle your blade and refuse to part.

toenail_sword.description: A giant toenail clipping, curved and razor-sharp along one edge. It's about the size of a short sword and, despite its unpleasant origin, is perfectly serviceable as a weapon. The keratin is surprisingly hard.

hidden.description: Whether the clipping is still buried in the rubbish pile.

check.note: Dig the sharp toenail clipping out of the rubbish without losing it.

success.narrative: You manage to work the clipping free of the rubbish, gripping it carefully. It's disgusting, but it will serve as a weapon.

failure.narrative: You grab for the clipping, but it shifts among the moldy sandwiches and loose corks, slipping out of reach. You'll have to try again.

rusty_key.description: A massive iron key, rough with rust but still solid. It's extremely heavy — carrying it between rooms is a serious workout. The key's size and shape suggest it belongs to an equally oversized padlock.
rusty_key.dragging_note: You struggle under the weight of the giant iron key, every step a laborious effort. The key drags behind you, scraping against the canvas floor.

axe_head.name: Axe Head
axe_head.description: You are standing on the head of a giant battleaxe, tilted at an angle against the canvas wall. The blade is wide enough to walk on, and the handle slopes downward into the murky darkness like a narrow sidewalk. Above you, just past head height, the canvas walls converge into a low dome, scrunching shut at the center. The walls emit a faint luminescence, enough to see by. Near the top of the blade, a small rip in the canvas reveals a sliver of gray light from outside.

exits.direction: Clamber carefully down the axe handle
Drop down into the darkness below

on_traverse.narrative: You steel yourself and step off the axe head into the darkness. The fall is terrifying — you plummet through the murky air before crashing into the pile of rubbish below. The impact is brutal, leaving you severely injured.

axe_handle_upper.name: Axe Handle (Upper)
axe_handle_upper.description: You are balancing carefully on the upper section of the giant axe handle. Here, a mass of sticky webs clings to the handle and nearby walls, greatly diminishing the glow from the canvas. You can barely make out your way forward. The handle continues both upward (toward the axe head) and downward into denser webs. Various strange masses of different sizes are caught in the webbing — some small, some worryingly large. It's also possible to drop down into the darkness below, though it's too dark to see where you'd land.

exits.direction: Climb up the axe handle toward the axe head
Climb down the axe handle toward the denser webs
Drop down into the darkness below

on_traverse.narrative: You drop from the handle into the darkness. You land hard on the pile of rubbish below, twisting your ankle in the process. Pain lances up your leg, but you can still move.

check.note: Always succeeds on first examination — the fly is found automatically.

success.narrative: As you examine the strange masses stuck in the webbing, one of them twitches. You lean closer and make out the form of a giant fly, about the size of a dog, its wings pinned by sticky strands. Its compound eyes swivel weakly toward you, and a faint, nasally groan escapes its mouthparts.

axe_handle_lower.name: Axe Handle (Lower)
axe_handle_lower.description: You are on the lower section of the axe handle, where the webs are very dense. Thick, sticky strands crisscross the path both upward and downward, making passage extremely difficult. The faint glow from the canvas walls barely penetrates here, casting everything in deep shadow. Peering over the side of the handle, you can make out numerous irregularly-shaped objects below — it looks like you could drop down safely from here.

exits.direction: Climb up the axe handle toward the upper section
Force your way through the dense webs upward

check.note: Force through the dense webbing upward.

traversal_check.failure_narrative: You strain against the sticky webs, but they hold fast. You can't break through — yet.

on_traverse.narrative: You burst through the sticky webs, tearing a path through the dense strands.

exits.direction: Walk down the axe handle to the bag base
Force your way through the dense webs downward

check.note: Force through the dense webbing. DC 14 bare-handed; the scenario calls for DC 10 with a weapon (see generation report).

traversal_check.failure_narrative: You strain against the sticky webs, but they hold fast. You can't break through — yet.

on_traverse.narrative: You burst through the sticky webs, tearing a path through the dense strands.

exits.direction: Drop safely down to the floor

on_traverse.narrative: You drop from the lower handle and land on the rubbish pile below. You're momentarily winded, but otherwise uninjured.

check.note: Notice the spider concealed in the shadows above.

success.narrative: Your eyes adjust to the gloom, and you catch a glint of movement above. Eight glittering eyes stare back at you from the shadows. A huge spider — easily twice your size — lurks in the webbing, watching your every move.

bag_floor.name: Bag Floor
bag_floor.description: You are on the floor of the cave, surrounded by a loose, sprawling pile of giant-sized rubbish. The faint glow of the canvas walls illuminates copper pieces like shields, empty potion bottles as tall as you, used corks like footstools, wads of lint, moldy sandwiches, and other, less identifiable items. The base of the giant battleaxe rests in the center of the pile, its handle stretching up into the darkness — you could clamber back up from here. Near one edge of the pile, a makeshift shelter of scrap metal and a giant overturned soup ladle looks distinctly... inhabited.

exits.direction: Squeeze through the concealed flap in the floor

on_traverse.narrative: You squeeze through the flap and drop down into a dark, cramped space.

on_enter.narrative: You land amidst the giant-sized debris, sending a copper piece spinning like a fallen shield. The smell of stale sandwiches and dwarf sweat hangs thick in the air.

secret_compartment.name: Secret Compartment
secret_compartment.description: You are in a tiny, closet-sized space beneath the canvas floor. The walls here are made of the same faintly-glowing canvas as the rest of the cave, but they feel even stickier and smell distinctly worse — like old socks and damp fabric. The only thing of note is a massive iron key, propped against one wall. It's rusty but solid, and almost half your height.

exits.direction: Squeeze back out to the Bag Floor

on_traverse.narrative: You squeeze back through the flap, emerging onto the Bag Floor.

spider_attack.description: The spider attacks when the player tries to force through the webs in Axe Handle (Lower). Resolved via Korbar's behavior.encounter_rules.

rules.narrative: Bare-handed, you are no match for the spider. It overpowers you with terrifying speed, and its venom quickly does its work. Everything goes dark.

on_success.narrative: You swing your weapon and land a solid blow! The spider hisses in pain, then scuttles away into the shadows, disappearing from the game.

on_failure.narrative: Your blow glances off the spider's tough carapace. It strikes back with lightning speed, fangs sinking deep. Venom floods your veins. Everything goes dark.

win_escape_bag.description: The player unlocks the padlock and opens the Bag from the inside, escaping into the world beyond.
win_escape_bag.narrative: With the padlock open, the neck of the Bag loosens. You push against the canvas and feel it give way. Real light — real air — streams in from above. You haul yourself up through the opening and tumble out onto solid ground, gasping with relief. You're free. Behind you, the Bag of Holding sits innocently on the floor, its secrets — and possibly a certain dwarf — still within.

lose_astral_plane.description: The player squeezes through the rip into the Astral Plane, drifting eternally in the gray void.
lose_astral_plane.narrative: You drift in the endless gray of the Astral Plane. The rip in the Bag — your only way back — is already out of reach, a tiny pinprick of light in an ocean of nothing. There is no up, no down, no sound but your own breathing. You are alone here, forever.

lose_key_lost.description: The player drops the giant key through the rip into the Astral Plane, making escape impossible.
lose_key_lost.narrative: You watch helplessly as the giant key tumbles through the rip and disappears into the gray void. It's gone — irretrievable, lost forever in the infinite expanse of the Astral Plane. Without that key, the padlock will never open. You are trapped in this Bag for the rest of your life, however long that may be.
